---
title: "Somalogic Batch Effect Test"
author: "Dylan Hirsch"
date: "4/1/2020"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = '/Volumes/SG_DATA/PROJECTS/Monogenic_Project/')
```

# Setup

We load libraries
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
source('scripts/util/Processing/averageRepeatSamples.R')
```


We extract data from the larger plates
```{r}
eset = readRDS('Data/Somalogic/data_analysis_ready/analysis_ready_sample_level_training_somalogic.rds')
plate.counts = table(eset$plate_id)
plates = names(plate.counts)#[plate.counts > 20]
#eset = eset[, eset$plate_id %in% plates]
```

We make sure there are no subjects with visits spread across plates
```{r}
df = data.frame(patient = eset$patient_id, plate = eset$plate_id)
df = unique(df)
patients.to.remove = df$patient[duplicated(df$patient)]
eset = eset[, !eset$patient_id %in% patients.to.remove]
```

We average over the repeat samples
```{r}
eset = averageRepeatSamples(eset, meta.cols = c('condition','gender','plate_id'))
```

For each parameter we test for the potential effect of batch
```{r}
X = t(exprs(eset))
df = data.frame(age = eset$Age, gender = as.numeric(factor(eset$gender)))
conditions = setdiff(eset$condition, 'Healthy')

for(condition in conditions) {
  df[condition] = as.numeric(eset$condition == condition)
}

for(plate in plates) {
  df[plate] = as.numeric(eset$plate_id == plate)
}

results = apply(X, 2, function(x) {
  model = lm(x ~ ., df)
  results = summary(model)
  results = results$coefficients
  return(results[, 4])
})

results = apply(results, 1, function(x) {
  p.adjust(x, method = 'fdr')
})
```

```{r}
results.2 = results[,25:31]
View(results.2[apply(results.2, 1, min) < .05,])
```
